title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 18)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A one-family antique in Andover, built in 1795, sold for $2,640,000, occupying a 55,191-square-foot lot.
A condo row-middle unit at 89 Beacon St., Boston, built in 1899, sold for $17,000,000, featuring 4 bedrooms and 8 baths.
A Colonial-style home in Amesbury, built in 1991, sold for $701,050, featuring 1,536 square feet and 3 bedrooms.

title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins' power play has plummeted to 12.8% success rate, ranking 28th in the NHL since the Olympic break.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have scored zero points at 5-on-5 play over the last 11 games for the Bruins.
Boston's third defensive pairing struggles, with Andrew Peeke rotated in and out, outscored 4-2 since the Milano-Cortina games.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit two crucial home runs, playing a key role in Venezuela's first World Baseball Classic title.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez pitched over four scoreless innings, contributing to Venezuela's championship victory.
Garrett Whitlock surrendered the winning run, while Roman Anthony struck out to end Team USA's World Baseball Classic run.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan from Ashland is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
Flanagan started running three years ago to improve her mental health and is now running to support families.
The Michael Lisnow Respite Center aids children with complex medical conditions and offers support to their families.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker convicted of involuntary manslaughter in death of 10-month-old foster child.
The child died from untreated complications of strep infection, bronchopneumonia, and empyema in February 2020.
The couple could face up to 20 years for manslaughter; sentencing is scheduled for March 19 in Berkshire County.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A water main break in Jackson Square flooded streets, causing traffic redirection and urging avoidance of the area.
MBTA bus routes 14, 22, 29, 41, and 44 were detoured; the Orange Line remained unaffected.
Three daycare businesses evacuated and closed, while crews worked to shut down the main and drain streets.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Broncos acquire Jaylen Waddle from Dolphins for three picks, including a 2026 first-rounder.
Patriots face a higher asking price for A.J. Brown trade after Broncos-Waddle deal.
Philadelphia likely firm on high trade demand for A.J. Brown amid Patriots' interest.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce and Maria Moraes charged after a Jeep hit three pedestrians outside Boston's ICON nightclub on Feb. 28.
Boyce faces charges of felony assault, reckless driving, and improper firearm storage; Moraes charged with disorderly conduct.
The trio victims reported the incident, leading to a quick arrest after a police stop and identification.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made an unscheduled appearance at the Irish American Partnership's St. Patrick’s Day Breakfast in Boston.
Biden, alongside former Ambassador Claire Cronin, addressed immigrant experiences, linking his ancestry to current immigration challenges.
Biden highlighted Boston’s Irish American history while urging optimism and unity among Americans amid immigration debates.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students were injured in a deck collapse during a party at 64 Undine Road, Brighton.
Police reported the deck's railing broke, causing the students to fall from the second floor onto concrete.
Inspectors found the deck's wood was rotting and painted over, and an investigation is ongoing.
